events.label: reasoning
events.text:
The scenario describes sawcut joints done late, resulting in random cracking. This is a defective condition in concrete finishing & curing. Sawcut contraction joints must be cut early enough (typically within 4-12 hours, before concrete reaches a certain strength) to control cracking. Cutting late means the concrete has already cracked randomly before the joints were introduced.

Key findings:
- Sawcut joints placed too late
- Random cracking has occurred (FD = field defect)
- This is non-compliant with ACI 302/308 guidance on joint timing

Decision: fail (non-compliant, defective work)
Risk: medium (random cracking compromises slab performance/aesthetics)
Work stage: rough-complete
Component conditions: installed-defective, non-compliant
Percent complete: ~85% (finishing done but defective)
Confidence: high ~0.9
